kill_fact: The catalyst is NOT a dated/binding event and the bear case that it is SOFT_EXTENDED is independently confirmed: BHC's exploration of a B+L SALE formally ended Feb 6, 2025 with no transaction (BHC kept its stake), and the remaining SPIN/Distribution has NO record date, NO outside date, and NO executed separation agreement. The Q1-2026 10-Q (period ended 3/31/2026) states only that 'The Distribution is subject to the achievement of targeted debt leverage ratios and the completion of the Separation is subject to the receipt of any applicable shareholder and other necessary approvals' — a leverage-gated, undated aspiration, not a binding instrument. Trigger sits OUTSIDE BLCO's control (parent's ~$20B+ debt / leverage test). This is a stage/watch asymmetry, NOT an aggressive in-window entry; the $27 clean-cap-table target is upside optionality, not a base case.
corrections: (1) STALE PRICE: catalyst live_price $15.08 is outdated — BLCO traded ~$14.87 as of Jun 19, 2026, so live downside-to-floor is ~$14.87->$11.58 (~22%), not 21% from $15.08. (2) OWNERSHIP DRIFT: catalyst cites BHC '~88% per Feb 11, 2026 10-K'; the Q1-2026 10-Q reports ~87% of BLCO shares as of Apr 22, 2026 (310.4M shares = ~88% as of Oct 22, 2025). Still a controlling ~87-88% block / thin float — directionally unchanged. (3) GATE FRAMING: the primary-source gate in the latest 10-Q is a 'targeted debt leverage ratios' test plus shareholder/other approvals; the catalyst's 'independent solvency opinion / Canadian closing condition' and 'Valeant fraudulent-transfer litigation' framing is NOT spelled out in the BLCO 10-Q separation disclosure (it is bull/bear narrative color, not a quoted BLCO filing condition) — treat the leverage-ratio + approvals gate as the verified blocker. (4) FLOOR: 52-week low confirmed at $11.58 (some sources $11.44); the ~$11.80 floor reference is sound.
evidence: Bausch Health update, announced Feb 6, 2025: board determined not to move forward with a B+L sale ('the process ... will not result in a transaction at this time'); BHC continues to own ~88% and pursue liability-management alternatives — Bloomberg/SEC 8-K coverage (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000885590/...ex991... ; bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-02-06/bausch-lomb-sinks-after-disclosing-it-won-t-go-private-now).

BLCO 10-Q (period ended 3/31/2026, sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001860742/000186074226000062/blco-20260331.htm): BHC held ~87% of BLCO common shares as of Apr 22, 2026; separation/Distribution disclosed as undated, 'subject to the achievement of targeted debt leverage ratios' and 'receipt of any applicable shareholder and other necessary approvals' — NO record date, NO outside date, NO executed separation agreement.

BLCO FY2025 10-K (filed Feb 18, 2026): 2025 revenue $5,101M (Vision Care 57% / Pharma 25% / Surgical 18%); BHC owned ~88% as of Feb 11, 2026; separation via distribution/sale/other structure 'with no assurance it will be completed' — confirms a real, growing $5.1B franchise underpinning the floor (ir.bausch.com 4Q25/FY25 results; stocktitan.net BLCO 10-K).

BLCO price/range as of Jun 19, 2026: ~$14.87, 52-week range ~$11.58-$18.92 — confirms the ~$11.58/$11.80 observed real-business floor and a now-lower live price than the catalyst's $15.08 (stockanalysis.com/stocks/blco/statistics; macrotrends.net/stocks/charts/BLCO).
